The Rising I-A: The Noble's Secret.name: The Rising I-A: The Noble's Secret
The Rising I-A: The Noble's Secret.questStatement: Your House parent has tasked you with victory in the Ludi Corvini and the protection of a dark family secret—but the festival offers a chance to rise above mere duty.
The Rising I-A: The Noble's Secret.mainObjective: Compete in the Ludi Corvini Games and navigate House politics to earn the notice of a powerful patron.
The Rising I-A: The Noble's Secret.completionCondition: Player has competed in noble-affiliated games (chariot race, dueling competition, or spectacle), navigated House secrets successfully, and caught the attention of a significant patron or House rival through an impressive or defiant act.
The Rising I-A: The Noble's Secret.questDesignBrief: For Noble-origin characters. The Games themselves (chariot dueling in the Circus, display of furycraft in the Sacred Way, or House-sanctioned combat) are the surface quest. Below runs the hidden thread: rumors of wild-born kin in House Aurelius, House Crassus debts, House Drusus barbarian talks, or House Tullius hidden colony. The player must balance personal performance with protecting House interests, earning the confidence of a House leader (Titus Aurelius for Aurelius, Crispus Crassus for Crassus, etc.). The climax is the player's shining moment: a daring move in the Games, a brilliant political maneuver, or a defiant choice that seizes the attention of a major patron—Titus Aurelius himself, Crispus Crassus, or a rival House leader. The player's rise from dutiful heir to noticed contender is sealed.

The Rising I-B: The Initiate's Trial.name: The Rising I-B: The Initiate's Trial
The Rising I-B: The Initiate's Trial.questStatement: You have been chosen for the most dangerous honor—the public demonstration of furycraft before the Emperor himself, but saboteurs aim to ensure your death.
The Rising I-B: The Initiate's Trial.mainObjective: Survive the sacred furycraft demonstrations in the Sacred Way and impress the Emperor and Magister Aurelius through furycraft mastery.
The Rising I-B: The Initiate's Trial.completionCondition: Player has survived the furycraft demonstration gauntlet; has identified and confronted or overcome at least one saboteur; has demonstrated exceptional furycraft power in front of the Emperor and high society.
The Rising I-B: The Initiate's Trial.questDesignBrief: For Collegium-origin characters. The demonstration is a structured furycraft battle in the Sacred Way before the Emperor and thousands of witnesses. Six students (one per numen) demonstrate simultaneously. The player quickly realizes that at least one 'opponent' is a trained saboteur aimed at their death. This is Magister Aurelius's gambit: by publicly demonstrating the player's furycraft gift, he shields them from quiet assassination and forces the saboteurs into the open where consequences are real. The player must identify the saboteurs (they may come from House Aurelius, House Crassus, House Varro), navigate the political fallout, and emerge with Magister Aurelius's protection—a powerful ally who sees them as exceptional and bound to greater things. The climax is the player's triumph in the demonstration: a daring furycraft display, overcoming a saboteur in full view, or a moment of raw power that impresses the Emperor himself. The player's rise from promising student to noticed prodigy is sealed, and Magister Aurelius becomes a patron who will defend their interests.

The Rising I-C: The Gladiator's Bargain.name: The Rising I-C: The Gladiator's Bargain
The Rising I-C: The Gladiator's Bargain.questStatement: You are condemned, promised freedom for five wins in the arena—but the fights are rigged, and powerful factions are watching to see what you are truly made of.
The Rising I-C: The Gladiator's Bargain.mainObjective: Survive five consecutive duels in the Circus Aquilae and earn the respect and notice of a powerful patron through combat prowess or cunning.
The Rising I-C: The Gladiator's Bargain.completionCondition: Player has won all five duels; has navigated the conspiracies surrounding the arena; has impressed or forged an alliance with a significant patron (Gavius, a House leader, or other influential figure).
The Rising I-C: The Gladiator's Bargain.questDesignBrief: For Gladiator-origin characters. The five duels are real combat challenges against skilled opponents drawn from various factions—some are Corvid Guard, some are House-chosen fighters, some are gladiator veterans with their own agendas. Gavius knows much of this and must decide where his loyalty lies. The player must read Gavius's tension and win his trust through victories in the sand, clever negotiations, or shrewd reads of his character. The climax is not a shared choice about a fugitive, but the player's shining moment in the arena: a defiant victory on the final match, a spectacular display of martial skill, or a brilliant political maneuver (perhaps secretly aiding a rival to the Guard, or refusing to kill an opponent who shows honor). Whatever the act, it catches the eye of a major patron—Gavius himself becomes a true ally, or a House leader notices the player's mettle and reaches out with offers of patronage. The player's rise from condemned to noticed contender is sealed.

The Rising I-D: The Gutter Shadow's Hunt.name: The Rising I-D: The Gutter Shadow's Hunt
The Rising I-D: The Gutter Shadow's Hunt.questStatement: Your handler has tasked you with a delicate job during the festival—theft, eavesdropping, or delivery in the crowd—but the job is more complicated than it first appears.
The Rising I-D: The Gutter Shadow's Hunt.mainObjective: Complete Kessa's festival assignment and uncover the truth behind the task.
The Rising I-D: The Gutter Shadow's Hunt.completionCondition: Player has completed Kessa's assigned job (theft, eavesdropping, delivery, or sabotage); has discovered unexpected leverage or a patron's attention as a result; has proven their worth and cunning to Kessa or another influential figure.
The Rising I-D: The Gutter Shadow's Hunt.questDesignBrief: For Gutter-origin characters. This is a surveillance and espionage quest, but not hunting a fugitive. Instead, the player uses street networks, crowd-reading, and Subura knowledge to complete a task Kessa assigns: steal a particular item from a House noble's tent, eavesdrop on a secret conversation in the Circus crowd, deliver a mysterious package to a contact, or sabotage a rival's chariot. As the player moves through the festival, they begin to realize the true scope: multiple factions are in motion (the Corvid Guard, Houses Aurelius and Crassus, House Tullius), and the player's job is a small cog in a larger game. Kessa is quietly investigating leverage and power. The climax is the player's moment of insight and action: they discover unexpected information (a hidden alliance, a scandal, a plot), they exploit it cleverly, they turn the tables on their employer, or they make a decisive move that catches a patron's attention. Perhaps they extract themselves from Kessa's reach and enter the orbit of House Tullius or the Unbound. Perhaps they gain leverage over Kessa herself. Whatever the outcome, the player's rise from street agent to noticed player is sealed—they've proven themselves too clever or too valuable to remain gutter-bound.

The Rising I: Four Convergences.name: The Rising I: Four Convergences
The Rising I: Four Convergences.questStatement: Four separate paths through the Grand Ludi Corvini converge in a single truth: each of you has been noticed by the powerful, and your rise has only just begun.
The Rising I: Four Convergences.mainObjective: Complete your origin trial and seize your moment in the Ludi Corvini to earn a patron's attention.
The Rising I: Four Convergences.completionCondition: Player has completed the Ludi Corvini trials; has caught the notice of a significant patron or House leader; has established themselves as a rising power worthy of the succession game.
The Rising I: Four Convergences.questDesignBrief: This is the convergence point of four origin tracks. Each origin (Noble, Initiate, Gladiator, Gutter) runs separately through Acts I-A to I-D, with each origin's quest culminating in a personal moment of notice: the Noble's daring play in the Games, the Initiate's triumph in the furycraft demonstration, the Gladiator's defiant victory in the arena, or the Gutter-shadow's clever exploitation of opportunity. These are not shared choices about a single target; they are four separate moments of elevation. The convergence is the recognition that all four have been elevated simultaneously. The Ludi Corvini itself—the games, demonstrations, duels, and crowd chaos—is the stage where these moments crystallize. By the end of Act I, each player has a patron (Titus Aurelius, Magister Aurelius, Gavius, Kessa, or a House rival), a reputation earned through their own deeds, and a secure foothold in the succession game. The stage is set for Act II's intrigue among the great powers.

The Rising II: The Succession Shadow.name: The Rising II: The Succession Shadow
The Rising II: The Succession Shadow.questStatement: Having proven yourself in the Ludi, you are drawn into the imperial succession crisis—caught between golden Titus Aurelius and ruthless Crispus Crassus, each one Fury shy of the throne. The Emperor watches to see who you truly serve, and the Ten Houses scheme to court or destroy you.
The Rising II: The Succession Shadow.mainObjective: Navigate the succession war by accumulating leverage, allies, and secrets; court the Houses and become a power broker without committing fully to either Aurelius or Crassus.
The Rising II: The Succession Shadow.completionCondition: Player has aligned strategically with multiple factions without resolving the succession; has uncovered at least three major political secrets; has amassed a personal faction or patronage network of at least five significant NPCs from different Houses; the succession remains unresolved at Act II's end.
The Rising II: The Succession Shadow.questDesignBrief: Act II is a sprawling political intrigue arc set on the Palatine, in the Forum, and at the Corvine Palace itself. The player rises from newly-noticed to genuine power broker as they navigate between Titus Aurelius (offering prestige, legitimacy, and popular support—the golden heir) and Crispus Crassus (offering wealth, leverage through debt, and merchant backing—the cold acquisitor). Each is five-Fury strong; the Emperor alone holds all six. Meanwhile, the Emperor orchestrates events from shadows, testing where the player's loyalty lies. The Ten Swing Houses offer their own opportunities: House Drusus (war and northern ties), House Sabinus (rivalry with Drusus), House Pontius (visionary eastern alternative), House Ventidius (naval ambitions), House Murena (shadow intelligence), House Tullius (wild-born sympathizer and upstart), House Serenus (neutral broker), House Fabius (paranoid ward-keepers), House Varro (keeper of secrets), and the Aurelius family itself. The player should uncover threads of conspiracy: the Aurelius wild-born scandal (growing to breaking point), the Crassus ledger of bought senators, House Fabius's secret wards (and what they guard), the Hollow Choir's infiltration, and whispers of something ancient stirring beneath. By Act II's end, the player is powerful enough that their choice of allegiance will shape the succession itself—but the player must not resolve it yet. The succession war simmers, unresolved. Act II climaxes with either a revelation that forces the player's hand (the wild-born scandal breaks, or a Swarm tremor shakes the capital) or a political event that makes clear the succession cannot be decided by scheming alone.

The Rising III: The War Far Away.name: The Rising III: The War Far Away
The Rising III: The War Far Away.questSource: Admiral Octavius Ventidius or House War Council
The Rising III: The War Far Away.questStatement: The Imperium's succession crisis is pushed aside when the player is dispatched to the eastern wars—the siege of Portus Aurorae becomes a crucible where real power is forged, and where the first cracks in the ancient seals begin to show.
The Rising III: The War Far Away.mainObjective: Assume command of Aquilan forces in the Tharsi war; break the siege of Portus Aurorae or secure a strategic breakthrough that shifts the balance of power.
The Rising III: The War Far Away.completionCondition: Player has participated in major military engagements in the Contested Coast region; has discovered evidence of the Buried Swarm or the failing wards; has gained real military authority commanding hundreds or thousands of soldiers.
The Rising III: The War Far Away.questDesignBrief: Act III shifts the arena of power from court to battlefield. The player is sent (by their patron or by the Emperor himself) to command forces in the Contested Coast region, where Aquilan furycraft clashes with Tharsi blood-magic. The siege of Portus Aurorae is grinding and brutal—neither side can break through, food is scarce, desertion runs high, and victory feels impossible. Here the player earns scars, commands respect through hard decisions, and accumulates a personal following of soldiers and mages loyal to them personally, not to their House. The war also serves a deeper purpose: Portus Aurorae sits atop or near geologically sensitive areas. As the player operates in the region, they begin to notice tremors, strange phenomena at night, and whispered reports of horrors at the war's fringes. Scouts report massive, insectile creatures moving in sealed underground passages. Battle sites erupt with impossible violence. The player can piece together that something far older than the Tharsi threat lurks beneath—and that the war may be weakening the seals that bind it. Meanwhile, the succession crisis smolders across the Mare Internum: Titus Aurelius and Crispus Crassus both seek to reach into the war through their agents, Ventidius and Murena feud openly, and Tharsis itself fractures between merchant-princes seeking peace and blood-priests demanding victory. By Act III's end, the player should have military command, a personal army or faction, evidence of the Buried Swarm, and urgent knowledge that the Imperium's politics matter far less than the existential threat awakening beneath.

The Rising IV: The Buried Swarm Wakes.name: The Rising IV: The Buried Swarm Wakes
The Rising IV: The Buried Swarm Wakes.questSource: Emperor Tiberius Corvus, House Fabius, or the Player's Own Realization
The Rising IV: The Buried Swarm Wakes.questStatement: The seals break. The ancient hive-dread known as the Buried Swarm erupts from the depths. The fractured realm—Houses at war, the succession unresolved, the Tharsi still pressing—must unite or be devoured. Only the player, bound by no House orthodoxy, can rally them.
The Rising IV: The Buried Swarm Wakes.mainObjective: Unite the fractured Imperium against the Buried Swarm; raid the sealed vaults beneath the realm; discover the true nature of the Six Furies and the purpose they were forged to serve; strike the killing blow that seals or defeats the Swarm.
The Rising IV: The Buried Swarm Wakes.completionCondition: Player has rallied at least three major Houses to the fight; has ventured into the Buried Deep; has confronted the Swarm's manifestations; has survived the final confrontation and determined the Swarm's fate (sealed, defeated, or transformed).
The Rising IV: The Buried Swarm Wakes.questDesignBrief: Act IV is the catastrophic finale where the Buried Swarm's awakening forces the succession to its conclusion. The Swarm's emergence is not sudden but a rising dread—tremors grow worse, creatures emerge at night, the wards visibly fail. The Emperor is forced to reveal what he has long guarded: the Six Furies were forged to cage this thing, generations ago, by the founders of the Imperium. The Houses must choose: unite behind one leader (likely the player, now known and respected across the realm), or be devoured separately. The player must: (1) rally the Houses—a political feat requiring all the leverage accumulated across Acts I-III; (2) coordinate military forces—marshaling the armies of multiple Houses into unified action; (3) delve into the Buried Deep with chosen allies (Magister Aurelius for furycraft secrets, Gavius for tactical command, Kessa for infiltration, military leaders, sympathetic mages) to reach the Swarm's heart; (4) uncover the Swarm's true nature (an ancient hive-intelligence, perhaps pre-dating civilization, perhaps aware and communicative); (5) confront it directly. The finale can take multiple forms depending on player choices: seal it again through ritual furycraft (requiring sacrifice or the cooperation of the Six Furies' masters, including the Emperor), defeat it through martial might (requiring the united Aquilan forces at full strength), negotiate with it (if it proves partially sentient), or transform the seals into something new. The succession is finally resolved as the price of unity—the Houses agree upon a new leader or restructure the throne entirely. The player's unique position as House-unbound is the key to the finale—they alone can speak to gutter and throne alike, binding them in common cause. By the end, the Imperium is forever changed. The succession is either resolved, transcended, or reset. The player's personal rise culminates with them as the unlikely linchpin of civilization's survival.

D-Rank Advancement.name: D-Rank Advancement
D-Rank Advancement.questStatement: You have signed on with the Free Companies at the bronze rank, and the contract boards are open to you.
D-Rank Advancement.mainObjective: Report three completed contracts at any Free Companies chapterhouse to earn the silver tag of C-Rank.
D-Rank Advancement.questDesignBrief: A progression track for newly-enrolled D-Rank companions. The player takes low-stakes contracts from the D-Rank board -- pest-culling, escort runs, minor investigations -- and reports each at a chapterhouse. Three reported contracts promote them to C-Rank. Keep the work grounded and achievable; this is the bottom rung where a name is first earned.

C-Rank Advancement.name: C-Rank Advancement
C-Rank Advancement.questStatement: Silver hangs at your throat; the Companies trust you to range alone, and the road to steel is five contracts long.
C-Rank Advancement.mainObjective: Report five more completed contracts at any Free Companies chapterhouse to earn the steel tag of B-Rank.
C-Rank Advancement.questDesignBrief: A progression track for C-Rank companions ranging the realm alone. Work is drawn from the C-Rank board -- bandit crews, dangerous beasts, furycraft disturbances, escorts through contested country -- and often requires travel between regions. Five further reported contracts promote them to B-Rank.

B-Rank Advancement.name: B-Rank Advancement
B-Rank Advancement.questStatement: The steel tag marks you a proven professional; five hard contracts more, and the gold of A-Rank is yours.
B-Rank Advancement.mainObjective: Report five more completed contracts at any Free Companies chapterhouse to earn the gold tag of A-Rank.
B-Rank Advancement.questDesignBrief: A progression track for B-Rank professionals taking reputation-making work from the B-Rank board -- monster incursions, regional feuds, complex furycraft anomalies, high-value escorts. Five further reported contracts elevate them to A-Rank, the highest standing the Companies grant from service.

The Free Companies I: The Undercut Contr.name: The Free Companies I: The Undercut Contract
The Free Companies I: The Undercut Contr.questStatement: Contracts are vanishing from the Muster boards, companions go unpaid, and a magistrate's writ now hangs on the chapterhouse door questioning the guild's right to muster armed men on Crassan ground.
The Free Companies I: The Undercut Contr.mainObjective: Investigate the sabotage of the Grand Chapterhouse and uncover who is building a case against the Free Companies.
The Free Companies I: The Undercut Contr.completionCondition: Player has investigated the vanishing contracts and unpaid chits, traced the discrepancies to doctored books, and discovered that quartermaster Grendus is keeping a false set of ledgers for an unknown paymaster.
The Free Companies I: The Undercut Contr.questDesignBrief: Opening of the Free Companies' home-defense arc, for an established member at the Mercenary Muster HQ. The surface trouble is mundane and infuriating: contracts pulled from the boards, wages 'lost,' and a magistrate's writ challenging the guild's charter-ground. Brennus, the First Sword, sets the player to find the rot. The trail runs through underpaid companions and mismatched chits to the Armory and Stores, where the quartermaster Grendus keeps a locked back room -- and a second, false ledger. The climax is the discovery that Grendus is cooking the Muster's books to manufacture a case against the guild, though for whom is not yet clear. Ties to Grendus, Brennus, House Crassus, and the Muster's long tension with its Crassan hosts.

The Free Companies II: The Lever and the.name: The Free Companies II: The Lever and the Hand
The Free Companies II: The Lever and the.questStatement: Grendus's false ledgers lead to a Crassus factor in the Counting-House who is assembling a dossier to prove before the Senate that the Free Companies are an unlicensed private army.
The Free Companies II: The Lever and the.mainObjective: Expose the conspiracy: turn or break quartermaster Grendus, and seize or destroy Factor Vibius Naevius's dossier in the Crassan Counting-House.
The Free Companies II: The Lever and the.completionCondition: Player has confronted Grendus and learned he was bought through a called-in debt; has penetrated the Crassan Counting-House; and has obtained, copied, destroyed, or turned Factor Vibius Naevius's dossier against the Free Companies.
The Free Companies II: The Lever and the.questDesignBrief: The layered middle act. Grendus is the lever; behind him stands Vibius Naevius, a cold Crassus factor in the Crassan Counting-House assembling a meticulous legal case -- doctored musters, false contracts, bought testimony -- to have the Senate brand the Free Companies a private army. Behind Naevius stands Crispus Crassus, whose name is kept off every page. The player works both ends: frighten or bargain Grendus into flipping (he keeps his true accounting hidden in the false ledger's binding) and gather counter-evidence, including Decima Naso's withheld proof of Crassus's false contracts, then penetrate the Counting-House to seize, copy, or destroy the dossier chained to Naevius's wrist. The act ends with the conspiracy laid bare -- but the legal threat now headed for the Senate floor, beyond the reach of any blade. Ties to the Crassus ledger of bought senators.

The Free Companies III: The Charter on T.name: The Free Companies III: The Charter on Trial
The Free Companies III: The Charter on T.questStatement: House Crassus moves in the Senate to brand the Free Companies a private army and dissolve their charter; Brennus will not bend, and the guild's right to exist beholden to none falls to the player to defend.
The Free Companies III: The Charter on T.mainObjective: Defend the Free Companies' charter before the Senate in the Forum and preserve the guild's neutrality against House Crassus.
The Free Companies III: The Charter on T.completionCondition: Player has marshaled evidence and allies, countered the Crassus motion in the Curia, and secured -- or transformed -- the Free Companies' charter so the guild remains beholden to no House.
The Free Companies III: The Charter on T.questDesignBrief: The climax, where the stakes rise from a single chapterhouse to the guild's very right to exist. House Crassus, through senators bought via the great ledger, moves in the Curia of the Forum to declare the Free Companies an unlicensed private army, their charter dissolved and their muster-grounds forfeit. Brennus refuses to bend and stakes the guild on the player. The player must defend the charter: marshal the evidence gathered in Act II, expose the bought senators, and rally the powers who would far rather the Companies stay neutral than become a Crassus army -- House Tullius and the Unbound (natural sympathizers), House Serenus the broker (who profits from balance), and rivals who fear a Crassus monopoly of swords. The player may sway the Rostra and the mob, cut a deal, or strike decisively. The resolution preserves or transforms the guild's neutrality and delivers Brennus's reckoning with the reach of Crassus -- and quietly marks the Free Companies as a player in the larger succession to come. Ties to Crispus Crassus, the Crassus ledger, House Tullius, the Unbound, and the Senate.

Toward the Sixth.name: Toward the Sixth
Toward the Sixth.questStatement: You would command more than the one or two furies your blood was meant for -- but the road toward the Emperor's mastery of all Six is lonely, perilous, and jealously guarded.
Toward the Sixth.mainObjective: Undertake the rite of the Widening Bond at the Collegium and awaken command of the numina beyond your natural gift.
Toward the Sixth.completionCondition: Player has sought out the deep binding-lore, survived the rite of widening -- a trial of will and Vis that opens broader command of the numina -- and taken the first real step toward mastery of many furies.
Toward the Sixth.questDesignBrief: The single furycraft-mastery questline of the generalist path, for a crafter who would command and combine many furies rather than perfect one. Magister Aurelius, master of Binding Theory, reluctantly shares the near-forbidden rite of widening one's bonds beyond the natural one or two -- the lonely road toward the all-six mastery only the Emperor holds, and the gateway to ALL furyweaving. The rite is a trial of will and Vis: the player opens themselves to numina their blood never bonded, risking backlash and ruin. Success grants the trait 'The Widening Bond' (a base in the Furyweaving skill and a deepening of every fury), which is the sole unlock for every weaving art. Thereafter the player learns them purely by skill: the general synthesis arts (Vis Confluence, The Shifting Bond, Twinned Working, Manifold Manifestation) as Furyweaving rises, and the great paired fusions -- forgefire (Ignis+Ferrum), stormtide (Ventus+Aqua), the living wall (Terra+Silva) -- for any two furies they have truly mastered plus Furyweaving. This is the high-ceiling, high-difficulty road: to wield many fusions one must spread skill across many furies AND climb the hard Furyweaving discipline. Weave in real danger: the Houses would kill to keep this art from spreading, for a wild-born or outsider who can widen and weave their own bonds threatens the whole bred monopoly on furystrength. Ties to the Collegium, the Citizenry and breeding lore, and the Emperor's unique mastery.

The Terra Master's Rite.name: The Terra Master's Rite
The Terra Master's Rite.questStatement: You would master Terra, the fury of earth and stone, to its deepest art -- the single-fury path, forgoing the furyweaver's breadth for unmatched depth in one numen.
The Terra Master's Rite.mainObjective: Complete the Terra Master's Rite at the Collegium and learn the master's secret arts of Terra.
The Terra Master's Rite.completionCondition: Player has undertaken the Terra Master's Rite, proven deep mastery of Terra alone, and been taught the single-fury master arts -- Stoneflesh and The Sundering -- that breadth-seeking furyweavers never learn.
The Terra Master's Rite.questDesignBrief: The specialist path for a crafter who perfects the single numen of Terra (earth and stone) rather than combining many. At the Collegium's Terra shrine in the Hall of Six, a master praeceptor teaches the deepest single-fury arts -- Stoneflesh and The Sundering -- reached only by a dedicated specialist. Success grants the trait 'Terra Master', which unlocks those elite abilities as the player's Terra skill climbs (25 for Stoneflesh, 35 for The Sundering). The trade against the furyweaver's path: a specialist's ceiling is one element mastered utterly, with no fusion, but their signature single-fury arts are unmatched and reached without the punishing spread of skill the furyweaver needs. Ties to the Collegium and the single-fury tradition.

The Ventus Master's Rite.name: The Ventus Master's Rite
The Ventus Master's Rite.questStatement: You would master Ventus, the fury of wind and lightning, to its deepest art -- the single-fury path, forgoing the furyweaver's breadth for unmatched depth in one numen.
The Ventus Master's Rite.mainObjective: Complete the Ventus Master's Rite at the Collegium and learn the master's secret arts of Ventus.
The Ventus Master's Rite.completionCondition: Player has undertaken the Ventus Master's Rite, proven deep mastery of Ventus alone, and been taught the single-fury master arts -- Skyborne and Thunderhead -- that breadth-seeking furyweavers never learn.
The Ventus Master's Rite.questDesignBrief: The specialist path for a crafter who perfects the single numen of Ventus (wind and lightning) rather than combining many. At the Collegium's Ventus shrine in the Hall of Six, a master praeceptor teaches the deepest single-fury arts -- Skyborne and Thunderhead -- reached only by a dedicated specialist. Success grants the trait 'Ventus Master', which unlocks those elite abilities as the player's Ventus skill climbs (25 for Skyborne, 35 for Thunderhead). The trade against the furyweaver's path: a specialist's ceiling is one element mastered utterly, with no fusion, but their signature single-fury arts are unmatched and reached without the punishing spread of skill the furyweaver needs. Ties to the Collegium and the single-fury tradition.

The Ignis Master's Rite.name: The Ignis Master's Rite
The Ignis Master's Rite.questStatement: You would master Ignis, the fury of flame and passion, to its deepest art -- the single-fury path, forgoing the furyweaver's breadth for unmatched depth in one numen.
The Ignis Master's Rite.mainObjective: Complete the Ignis Master's Rite at the Collegium and learn the master's secret arts of Ignis.
The Ignis Master's Rite.completionCondition: Player has undertaken the Ignis Master's Rite, proven deep mastery of Ignis alone, and been taught the single-fury master arts -- Heartkindler and The Unquenchable -- that breadth-seeking furyweavers never learn.
The Ignis Master's Rite.questDesignBrief: The specialist path for a crafter who perfects the single numen of Ignis (flame and passion) rather than combining many. At the Collegium's Ignis shrine in the Hall of Six, a master praeceptor teaches the deepest single-fury arts -- Heartkindler and The Unquenchable -- reached only by a dedicated specialist. Success grants the trait 'Ignis Master', which unlocks those elite abilities as the player's Ignis skill climbs (25 for Heartkindler, 35 for The Unquenchable). The trade against the furyweaver's path: a specialist's ceiling is one element mastered utterly, with no fusion, but their signature single-fury arts are unmatched and reached without the punishing spread of skill the furyweaver needs. Ties to the Collegium and the single-fury tradition.

The Aqua Master's Rite.name: The Aqua Master's Rite
The Aqua Master's Rite.questStatement: You would master Aqua, the fury of water, ice, and healing, to its deepest art -- the single-fury path, forgoing the furyweaver's breadth for unmatched depth in one numen.
The Aqua Master's Rite.mainObjective: Complete the Aqua Master's Rite at the Collegium and learn the master's secret arts of Aqua.
The Aqua Master's Rite.completionCondition: Player has undertaken the Aqua Master's Rite, proven deep mastery of Aqua alone, and been taught the single-fury master arts -- The Lifemender and Glamourweave -- that breadth-seeking furyweavers never learn.
The Aqua Master's Rite.questDesignBrief: The specialist path for a crafter who perfects the single numen of Aqua (water, ice, and healing) rather than combining many. At the Collegium's Aqua shrine in the Hall of Six, a master praeceptor teaches the deepest single-fury arts -- The Lifemender and Glamourweave -- reached only by a dedicated specialist. Success grants the trait 'Aqua Master', which unlocks those elite abilities as the player's Aqua skill climbs (25 for The Lifemender, 35 for Glamourweave). The trade against the furyweaver's path: a specialist's ceiling is one element mastered utterly, with no fusion, but their signature single-fury arts are unmatched and reached without the punishing spread of skill the furyweaver needs. Ties to the Collegium and the single-fury tradition.

The Ferrum Master's Rite.name: The Ferrum Master's Rite
The Ferrum Master's Rite.questStatement: You would master Ferrum, the fury of metal and battle, to its deepest art -- the single-fury path, forgoing the furyweaver's breadth for unmatched depth in one numen.
The Ferrum Master's Rite.mainObjective: Complete the Ferrum Master's Rite at the Collegium and learn the master's secret arts of Ferrum.
The Ferrum Master's Rite.completionCondition: Player has undertaken the Ferrum Master's Rite, proven deep mastery of Ferrum alone, and been taught the single-fury master arts -- The Unbreakable and Bladelord -- that breadth-seeking furyweavers never learn.
The Ferrum Master's Rite.questDesignBrief: The specialist path for a crafter who perfects the single numen of Ferrum (metal and battle) rather than combining many. At the Collegium's Ferrum shrine in the Hall of Six, a master praeceptor teaches the deepest single-fury arts -- The Unbreakable and Bladelord -- reached only by a dedicated specialist. Success grants the trait 'Ferrum Master', which unlocks those elite abilities as the player's Ferrum skill climbs (25 for The Unbreakable, 35 for Bladelord). The trade against the furyweaver's path: a specialist's ceiling is one element mastered utterly, with no fusion, but their signature single-fury arts are unmatched and reached without the punishing spread of skill the furyweaver needs. Ties to the Collegium and the single-fury tradition.

The Silva Master's Rite.name: The Silva Master's Rite
The Silva Master's Rite.questStatement: You would master Silva, the fury of wood and the green-dark, to its deepest art -- the single-fury path, forgoing the furyweaver's breadth for unmatched depth in one numen.
The Silva Master's Rite.mainObjective: Complete the Silva Master's Rite at the Collegium and learn the master's secret arts of Silva.
The Silva Master's Rite.completionCondition: Player has undertaken the Silva Master's Rite, proven deep mastery of Silva alone, and been taught the single-fury master arts -- The Green Silence and Wildwake -- that breadth-seeking furyweavers never learn.
The Silva Master's Rite.questDesignBrief: The specialist path for a crafter who perfects the single numen of Silva (wood and the green-dark) rather than combining many. At the Collegium's Silva shrine in the Hall of Six, a master praeceptor teaches the deepest single-fury arts -- The Green Silence and Wildwake -- reached only by a dedicated specialist. Success grants the trait 'Silva Master', which unlocks those elite abilities as the player's Silva skill climbs (25 for The Green Silence, 35 for Wildwake). The trade against the furyweaver's path: a specialist's ceiling is one element mastered utterly, with no fusion, but their signature single-fury arts are unmatched and reached without the punishing spread of skill the furyweaver needs. Ties to the Collegium and the single-fury tradition.
